title: I Was Wrong. This Is a Historic Buying Opportunity.
summary:
Transcript:
One month ago, the market was panicking
over the Iran war and broken supply
chains. Today, it's verging on extreme
greed. But below all the headlines, big

because they see a massive opportunity
ahead. My name is Alex and I spent 8

changing which stocks are about to win
big, and I'll show you a huge mistake

valuable, so let's get right into it.
The CNN fear and greed index has been

That part is obvious. War, broken supply

he buys back in and when other massive

where I think the biggest opportunities
in the market are right now.
Accountability is important to me. So,
let's talk about my big mistake because
I owe you a real apology here. Let's
talk about CPUs. Traditional AI data
centers run roughly one CPU for every
eight GPUs. The GPUs do the heavy math
and the CPUs keep the traffic moving.
But Agentic AI flips the script. When a

over the course of a session. None of

aents, and the context management runs

Huang showed specific numbers at GTC
2026. 12,000 GPUs running at scale need
400,000 CPU cores running next to them.
That sounds like a 33:1 CPU toGPU ratio,
which is one reason why I think stocks

167 racks. Each Vera rack has 256 CPUs
and each CPU has 88 cores. So one rack

That means for every 167 GPU racks, you
actually only need 18 CPU racks or a
ratio of 9:1 GPU to CPU racks, not the
other way around. In chip terms, a data
center wants 4600 Vera CPUs for every
1,200 Reuben GPUs or one CPU for every
2.6 GPUs. Now, here was my mistake and
why I owe you an apology. In my opinion,
investors should care about the racks.
After all, data centers are built and
priced in terms of racks. And like I
just showed you, Agentic AI needs nine
times more GPU racks than CPU racks. But
I didn't think about it in terms of
chips. And AI data centers only need
three times more GPUs than CPUs if you

more CPUs in AI data centers than I

didn't cover AMD enough and I really

listen to your feedback in the comments
and I really should have. I apologize.
Full stop. So, let me put my ego aside

Data center revenue came in at $5.8

57%. Most of that revenue comes from

That means Meta gets almost a hundred

just $30 more than its current price.
That's the real cost of competing in

On their latest earnings call, Lisa Sue

That's a compound annual growth rate of
35% or roughly three times faster than

First, Intel joined Terafab, a $25

So, Apple coming back would be one of

This is not a done deal, but it is the

they're the only American-owned and
operated factory that can build some of

they might finally have the customers to

CPUs just got a lot bigger. For the last

according to Jensen, it takes around
400,000 CPU cores to support 12,000
Reuben GPUs.
136 cores per ARM chip times 60 chips

18 racks of Nvidia Vera CPUs. That
sounds way worse, but let me fix the

instead of falling into the same trap.
If we look at the actual chip counts
instead of just the racks, it takes 4600
Vera CPUs to support those 12,000 Reuben

just like I showed you before. But you

another way, ARM's new CPU is actually
much more powerful than Nvidia's. So
much so that you need almost 40% fewer
to run the same data centers. And that's
just versus Nvidia. It has roughly
double the performance per watt versus

per gawatt of data center compute. So
let's bring everything full circle. Meta

in data center infrastructure using
AMD's chips. If they used ARMs AGI CPUs

I'm not the only one who caught that

ARM's first and flagship customer for

ARM didn't just enter the CPU

let alone hardware firms. The reason the

goes back to what I said at the start of

ARM's problem isn't that nobody wants

money knows it. But if you think demand

now, there's one more bombshell I need
to walk you through. Now, to be clear,

verified. So, it could be nothing or it

found this video valuable, consider

it tells me to make more content like

models today. The version they plan to

Claude, you could do that same work on

It didn't come with a public model to

I'm watching what happens next. If
Anthropic, OpenAI or Google publish

in response, that means these claims are

When the cost of something drops, you
